subject: English
title: Iswaran the Storyteller – Hard Short Notes

key_points: The story highlights the psychological power of storytelling and imagination
Mahendra represents logic while Iswaran represents creativity and imagination
Iswaran’s exaggerated storytelling builds suspense and fascination
The ghost story gradually affects Mahendra’s mind despite his logical nature
The mysterious night incident becomes the climax of the narrative
Mahendra eventually leaves the place due to fear created by imagination
characters: Mahendra – A rational character whose imagination is influenced by fear
Iswaran – A symbolic representation of imagination and storytelling
themes: Power of storytelling in shaping human perception
literary_devices: Suspense – Gradual development of tension in the narrative
Hyperbole – Exaggerated descriptions in the elephant story
Imagery – Detailed visual descriptions of the ghost
Contrast – Between Mahendra’s logic and Iswaran’s imagination
exam_tips: Analyse the psychological conflict experienced by Mahendra
Explain how suspense is built through storytelling
Discuss the contrast between imagination and rational thinking
Highlight the significance of the climax and ending
